flagship_directive: Prove a matching full-class feasible upper bound at the same polynomial exponent, ideally removing the finite-VC logarithmic factor or showing it is unavoidable by a matching lower bound.
critique: The delivered result is not the originally advertised no-log full-class minimax theorem: the feasible upper bound is only over collections satisfying common foldwise nuisance-rate conditions, and it carries a finite-VC logarithmic multiplier. The note does, however, prove a full weak-overlap two-point lower bound at the corrected exponent using the optimized admissible weak-arm exponent beta_{alpha,gamma}, and it proves a data-only clipped-AIPW upper bound at the same polynomial exponent under explicit high-level nuisance conditions. The nuisance condition is high-level but not question-begging: the drift and empirical-process terms are derived rather than assumed, and the known-logging finite-cell witnesses are shown to satisfy it. Thus the proved contribution is a genuine corrected polynomial-rate bracket, not an exact no-log full-class minimax characterization.
